- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}
3	
4	Enforces project-wide constraints on Dockerfile `FROM` lines so that the `ARG`/`${RBF_IMAGE_n}` parameterization stays the sole source of base-image origin. Hygiene violations short-circuit before any build, in both the local {rbtgo_ark_kludge} path and the Cloud Build {rbtgo_ark_conjure} path.
5	
6	Rules apply to every line at column 0 starting with `FROM` followed by whitespace, after column-0 `#` comments are filtered out. A violation emits `«path»:«lineno»: «rule» — «offending line»` precision and aborts the operation ({rbbc_fatal}). Multi-stage `AS «name»` codas after token 2 are admissible — the rule constrains token 2 only. An empty Dockerfile, or one with no column-0 `FROM` line, passes silently; Docker handles its own rejection.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *No tab character anywhere on the FROM line*. Tabs frustrate downstream tooling that whitespace-tokenizes the line and have no semantic role in modern Dockerfile syntax.
10	
11	//axhos_step
12	. *Second whitespace-delimited token is exactly* `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`*,* `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`*,* `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`*, or* `scratch` *(lowercase only)*. Load-bearing — this keeps {rbrv_image_origin} declarations the sole authority over what upstream a vessel pulls. Hardcoded literals (e.g. `python:3.12-slim`) bypass {rbrv_image_origin} and the {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} chain, defeating the supply-chain anchor that {rbtgo_ark_conjure} and {rbtgo_ark_kludge} rely on.
13	
14	//axhos_step
15	. *Line does not end with* `\`. Backslash continuations on `FROM` lines complicate the column-0 contract and carry no semantic value — the substantive directive fits on one line by construction.
16

19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Hygiene - Dockerfile FROM-line constraint shared by kludge and conjure
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBFH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBFH_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbfh_*)
29	
30	zrbfh_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBFH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfh already kindled"
32	  readonly ZRBFH_KINDLED=1
33	}
34	
35	zrbfh_sentinel() {
36	  test "${ZRBFH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfh not kindled - call zrbfh_kindle first"
37	}
38	
39	######################################################################
40	# External Functions (rbfh_*)
41	
42	# Dockerfile Hygiene Check
43	#
44	# Enforces project-wide constraints on Dockerfile FROM lines so that the
45	# ARG/${RBF_IMAGE_n} parameterization stays the sole source of base
46	# image origin. Hygiene violations short-circuit before any build, in
47	# both the local kludge and the Cloud Build conjure paths.
48	#
49	# Rules apply to every line at column 0 starting with FROM followed by
50	# whitespace, after column-0 `#` comments are filtered out. Violation
51	# emits {path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line} precision via buc_die.
52	# Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible. Empty
53	# Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently.
54	rbfh_dockerfile_check() {
55	  zrbfh_sentinel
56	
57	  local -r z_path="${1:-}"
58	  test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile path required"
59	  test -f "${z_path}" || buc_die "rbfh_dockerfile_check: Dockerfile not found: ${z_path}"
60	
61	  local z_line=""
62	  local z_lineno=0
63	  local z_token2=""
64	  local z_pre=""
65	  local z_post=""
66	
67	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
68	    z_lineno=$(( z_lineno + 1 ))
69	
70	    # Docker treats only column-0 `#` as a comment
71	    case "${z_line}" in
72	      '#'*) continue ;;
73	    esac
74	
75	    # Constrain only column-0 FROM lines followed by whitespace
76	    [[ "${z_line}" =~ ^FROM[[:space:]] ]] || continue
77	
78	    # Rule 1: no tab character anywhere on the FROM line
79	    case "${z_line}" in
80	      *$'\t'*) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line contains tab character — ${z_line}" ;;
81	    esac
82	
83	    # Rule 3: line does not end with `\`
84	    case "${z_line}" in
85	      *'\') buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM line ends with backslash continuation — ${z_line}" ;;
86	    esac
87	
88	    # Rule 2: second whitespace-delimited token must be approved
89	    read -r z_pre z_token2 z_post <<<"${z_line}"
90	    case "${z_token2}" in
91	      '${RBF_IMAGE_1}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_2}'|'${RBF_IMAGE_3}'|'scratch') : ;;
92	      *) buc_die "${z_path}:${z_lineno}: FROM image token must be \${RBF_IMAGE_1..3} or scratch — ${z_line}" ;;
93	    esac
94	  done < "${z_path}"
95	}
96	
97	# eof
98

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enumerates every
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by listing packages under `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/` via the GAR REST API implemented by
9	{gar_service},
10	classifying each
11	{rbtga_hallmark}
12	by basename presence under its subtree.
13	A single `packages.list` call returns the full corpus; no per-vessel iteration is required because vessel identity is no longer encoded in the GAR path.
14	
15	The
16	{rbtr_retriever}
17	tallies hallmarks:
18	
19	//axhos_step
20	. *Authenticate as
21	{rbtr_retriever}*:
22	.. Execute
23	{rbtoe_retriever_authenticate}
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«RETRIEVER_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Enumerate Hallmark Packages*:
29	.. {rbbc_store}
30	`«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}`)
31	.. {rbbc_store}
32	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
33	.. {rbbc_call}
34	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
35	... method: `GET`
36	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
37	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «RETRIEVER_TOKEN»`
38	.. {rbbc_require}
39	HTTP 200 response
40	.. Extract `.packages[].name` from the response, strip the resource prefix, and URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`
41	.. Filter to names whose path under `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/` is exactly two segments (`<hallmark>/<basename>`); excludes both shallower and deeper entries, and excludes anything outside `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/` (reliquaries, enshrines)
42	.. For each retained `<hallmark>/<basename>` package, {rbbc_call}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead: their parent manifest has been deleted by {rbtgo_image_delete} but their orphan children persist until the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*) reaps them on its next daily run. Filtering at this read site makes the display reflect the post-jettison user-facing state immediately, decoupling it from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
44	.. {rbbc_store}
45	`«PAIRS»` as sorted `<hallmark> <basename>` pairs (one per line) for packages with at least one live tag. An empty result is a valid outcome — no hallmarks in the repository.
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. *Classify Health Per Hallmark*:
49	.. Iterate `«PAIRS»` in sort order; each contiguous run of the same hallmark value constitutes one
50	{rbtga_hallmark}'s
51	ark inventory.
52	.. For each hallmark, record whether the basenames
53	{rbtga_ark_image},
54	{rbtga_ark_about},
55	and
56	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
57	each appear in its run. Other basenames (`pouch`, `diags`, `attest`) are retained for display but do not affect health classification.
58	.. Classify:
59	... `vouched`: `image` + `about` + `vouch` all present
60	... `pending`: `image` + `about` present, `vouch` absent
61	... `incomplete`: any other combination (including `image` without `about`, `about` without `image`, or only auxiliary basenames)
62	
63	//axhoot_typed_output
64	{rbtga_hallmark}
65	. *Display Table*:
66	.. {rbbc_show}
67	three-column header: `HALLMARK`, `HEALTH`, `BASENAMES`
68	.. For each hallmark (in sort order):
69	... {rbbc_show}
70	hallmark, health state, space-separated list of basenames present under its subtree
71	.. {rbbc_show}

73	
74	//axhos_step
75	. *Recommend Next Actions*:

77	... {rbbc_show}

79	.. If any `incomplete` hallmarks:

82	
83	//axhoc_completion
84	Procedure completes successfully when the packages listing returns and the classified table displays.
85	No resources are modified.

471	rbfl_tally() {
472	  zrbfl_sentinel
473	
474	  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
475	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
476	
477	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
478	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
479	    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
480	  local z_token=""
481	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") \
482	    || buc_die "Failed to get Retriever OAuth token"
483	
484	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
485	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
486	
487	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
488	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
489	    buc_success "Tally complete — 0 hallmarks"
490	    return 0
491	  fi
492	
493	  # Load-then-iterate. A synthetic sentinel element appended to the array
494	  # lets the final hallmark flush through the same boundary branch as every
495	  # intermediate one (single flush site).
496	  local z_lines=()
497	  local z_line=""
498	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
499	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
500	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
501	  z_lines+=("__SENTINEL__ __SENTINEL__")
502	
503	  echo ""
504	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "HALLMARK" "HEALTH" "BASENAMES"
505	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "------------------------------" "-----------" "---------"
506	
507	  # State machine over <hallmark> <basename> pairs (file was sorted by the
508	  # capture helper). Vessel is no longer encoded in the GAR path —
509	  # restoration via about/vouch metadata is AAL territory.
510	  local z_prev_h="" z_prev_bns=""
511	  local z_prev_img=0 z_prev_abt=0 z_prev_vch=0
512	  local z_count=0 z_vouched_n=0 z_pending_n=0 z_incomplete_n=0

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_abjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation formally renounces an
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	from
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by enumerating and deleting every package beneath the ark's
9	hallmark subtree via the GAR REST API implemented by
10	{gar_service}.
11	
12	An
13	{rbtga_ark}
14	is the coherent unit of a Recipe Bottle build: a set of packages rooted at
15	`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/` and distinguished by basename.
16	The
17	{rbtga_ark_image}
18	(basename `image`) contains the container image itself.
19	The
20	{rbtga_ark_about}
21	(basename `about`) contains SBOM and build metadata.
22	The
23	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
24	(basename `vouch`) contains verification results.
25	The
26	{rbtga_ark_pouch}
27	(basename `pouch`) contains the build context (conjure/bind only, absent for graft).
28	A fifth basename, `diags`, is present for conjure arks only (build-time diagnostics forwarded to the about pipeline).
29	For conjure vessels, a single `attest` package holds per-platform tags (`attest:«HALLMARK»-«ARCH»`).
30	Abjuring an ark deletes every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree as a single logical operation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	abjures an ark:
35	
36	//axhopt_typed_parameter
37	{rbtga_ark}
38	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	`«INPUT_VESSEL»` provided as first argument (retained for AAK signature compatibility; not used to construct the subtree path, drops in AAL)
41	.. {rbbc_require}
42	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` provided as second argument
43	.. {rbbc_fatal}
44	if hallmark missing: "Hallmark parameter required"
45	
46	//axhopt_typed_parameter
47	{rbtga_hallmark}
48	. *Validate Hallmark Format*:
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` matches pattern `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}` (mode prefix + dual timestamp)
51	.. {rbbc_fatal}
52	if malformed: "Hallmark format invalid"
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Authenticate as
56	{rbtr_director}*:
57	.. Execute
58	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
59	.. {rbbc_store}
60	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Construct Hallmark Subtree*:
64	.. {rbbc_store}
65	`«SUBTREE»` as `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»/` where `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` derives from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}`)
66	.. {rbbc_store}
67	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Enumerate Packages Under Subtree*:
71	.. {rbbc_call}
72	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
73	... method: `GET`
74	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
75	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
76	.. {rbbc_require}
77	HTTP 200 response
78	.. Extract `.packages[].name` from the response, strip the resource prefix, and URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«ARK_PACKAGES»` as the list of decoded package names whose prefix equals `«SUBTREE»`
81	.. {rbbc_fatal}
82	if `«ARK_PACKAGES»` is empty: "No packages found under «SUBTREE» — hallmark not present in registry"
83	
84	//axhos_step
85	. *Confirm Abjuration*:
86	.. {rbbc_show}
87	"Will abjure «COUNT» packages under «SUBTREE»:" followed by each entry in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`
88	.. {rbbc_require}
89	user types `yes` to confirm
90	.. {rbbc_fatal}
91	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
92	
93	//axhos_step
94	. *Delete Each Enumerated Package*:
95	.. For each `«PACKAGE»` in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`:
96	... {rbbc_store}
97	`«ENCODED»` as `«PACKAGE»` with `/` replaced by `%2F`
98	... {rbbc_call}
99	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/delete[GAR REST API: packages.delete] with:
100	.... method: `DELETE`
101	.... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages/«ENCODED»`
102	.... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
103	... {rbbc_require}
104	HTTP 200 response (deletion returns a long-running operation; the 200 is treated as accepted)
105	... {rbbc_fatal}
106	if delete fails: "Failed to delete package «PACKAGE» (HTTP «STATUS_CODE»)"
107	
108	//axhoot_typed_output
109	{rbtga_ark}
110	. *Display Results*:
111	.. {rbbc_show}
112	"Hallmark abjured: «INPUT_HALLMARK» («COUNT» packages)"
113	.. For each deleted package:
114	... {rbbc_show}
115	`  - «PACKAGE» deleted`
116	
117	//axhoc_completion
118	Procedure completes successfully when every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree has been deleted from the registry and the abjuration confirmation is displayed.
119	The ark is now formally renounced and no longer part of the repository corpus.
120	Because enumeration drives the delete set, there is no separate existence check and no orphan-detection branch: whatever lives in the subtree is removed, and an empty subtree is itself the "not present" signal.
121	Deleting a package removes all of its tags in one call — per-basename HEAD/DELETE dances are no longer required.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
5	{gar_service}
6	as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
7	The
8	{rbtr_director}
9	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each declared
10	{rbrv_image_origin}
11	from its upstream registry to GAR using
12	the reliquary skopeo image
13	(`skopeo copy --all`), preserving the manifest list and all per-platform manifests for multi-platform images.
14	The locator results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15	{rbrv_image_anchor}
16	is then written back to the vessel regime locally as a
17	{rbst_locator}.
18	
19	NOTE: This operation is a pure mirror.
20	Every call inspects current upstream for each declared
21	{rbrv_image_origin}
22	, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, copies the image into
23	{gar_service}
24	, and writes the resulting
25	{rbst_locator}
26	into the vessel's
27	{rbrv_image_anchor}
28	— overwriting whatever was previously committed there.
29	The
30	{rbrv_image_anchor}
31	is a derived artifact of this operation, not an operator-set pin.
32	Re-runs are idempotent with respect to upstream state, not with respect to the file's prior contents — when upstream rotates, the next call writes a different locator and that is the operation surfacing the change, not a fault.
33	
34	NOTE: Vessels whose
35	{rbrv_image_anchor}
36	is written by an upstream procedure as a hallmark-pin (e.g., the airgap-bottle pointing at the just-ordained forge image via `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»`) do not invoke this operation.
37	The orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) writes the locator directly;
38	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
39	resolves it at build time.
40	The hallmark's existence in
41	{gar_service}
42	is established by the producer's
43	{rbtgo_ark_ordain}
44	success, not by a separate validation step here.
45	
46	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
47	It must be run before
48	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
49	when anchored base images are desired.
50	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
51	
52	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
53	No local skopeo installation is required.
54	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
55	
56	NOTE: The enshrine Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the skopeo copy. Enshrine passes empty locator substitutions — enshrine's purpose is to create anchors, so pre-validating them would be circular.
57	
58	The enshrine operation:
59	
60	//axhopt_typed_parameter
61	{rbrv_sigil}
62	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
63	.. {rbbc_require}
64	vessel provided as first argument
65	.. {rbbc_require}
66	vessel declares at least one upstream base-image slot — that is, at least one of slots 1-3 has
67	{rbrv_image_origin}
68	populated.
69	{rbrv_image_anchor}
70	is computed by this operation; an ANCHOR-only slot is not a valid input shape
71	.. {rbbc_require}
72	vessel mode is `conjure` (`RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`)
73	.. {rbbc_require}
74	reliquary tool images exist in GAR (HEAD check on `docker:latest` canary)
75	
76	//axhos_step
77	. *Submit Enshrine Cloud Build Job*:
78	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
79	... Single step using
80	the reliquary skopeo image
81	as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
82	... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
83	... Mason SA, private pool, timeout
84	.. Submit via `builds.create` and wait for completion
85	.. The GCB step, for each non-empty ORIGIN slot:
86	... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
87	... {rbbc_store}
88	`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
89	... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
90	... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` yields anchor `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
91	... Composes the
92	{rbst_locator}
93	as `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` (e.g., `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/python-3.11-slim-abc123def4:python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`)
94	... Copies upstream image into the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
95	... {rbbc_require}
96	copy succeeds -- the locator now resolves to a permanent, content-addressed reference in
97	{gar_service}
98	containing all upstream platforms
99	... Single-platform upstream images are handled identically -- `skopeo copy --all` is a no-op on the platform dimension when only one platform exists
100	.. Writes JSON locator results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`); each slot's `anchor` field carries the full locator string
101	
102	//axhos_step
103	. *Extract Locators and Write Regime*:
104	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` from the completed build result (base64-decoded JSON)
105	.. For each slot present in the result:
106	... Write locator string to `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` in the vessel's {rbrv_regime} file, overwriting any prior value
107	
108	//axhoot_typed_output
109	{rbrv_image_anchor}
110	Per-slot locator string written to the vessel regime.
111	
112	//axhoc_completion
113	Successful execution produces anchored base image references in
114	{gar_service}
115	(with all upstream platforms preserved) and updates the vessel regime with
116	{rbrv_image_anchor}
117	values.
118	The vessel is now ready for reproducible
119	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
120	builds using GAR-mirrored base images.
121	Multi-platform conjure vessels pull the correct platform from the anchored manifest list automatically.
122	Re-running enshrine with the same
123	{rbrv_image_origin}
124	but a different upstream digest produces a new locator (upstream image was updated).
125	Re-running with the same digest is idempotent.
126

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_kludge}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, without authenticating to or fetching from Cloud Build or GAR. It produces a
5	{rbtga_hallmark}
6	with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark. Kludge resolves the same {rbrv_image_anchor} declarations that {rbtgo_ark_conjure} does — so an enshrined vessel built via kludge consumes the same GAR-anchored base layers as the conjure path — but anchored layers must be pre-cached locally; the credentialed fetch happens out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin-only slots (no anchor) require no credentials, so kludge pulls them inline if they're not already cached.
7	
8	NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.
9	
10	NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.
11	
12	The kludge operation:
13	
14	//axhopt_typed_parameter
15	{rbtga_ark}
16	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
17	.. {rbbc_require}
18	vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
19	.. {rbbc_require}
20	vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
21	.. {rbbc_require}
22	vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Resolve Base Images*:
26	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
27	{rbrv_image_origin}
28	is set:
29	... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is also set, resolve the slot's `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg to the full GAR reference composed from the locator (`«REGISTRY_HOST»/«PROJECT»/«REPOSITORY»/«PACKAGE_PATH»:«TAG»`) — same shape conjure produces
30	... If {rbrv_image_anchor} is empty, pass {rbrv_image_origin} through verbatim as the build-arg
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if a slot has {rbrv_image_anchor} set but {rbrv_image_origin} empty: "Malformed regime — anchor without origin"
33	
34	//axhos_step
35	. *Verify Local Image Presence*:
36	.. For each resolved slot ref, query the local container runtime (`docker image inspect`) to confirm the image is cached locally
37	.. On pass-through miss (origin without anchor): announce and run `docker pull «origin»` inline, then re-inspect to confirm the pull landed an image locally. No credentials are required for public upstream registries.
38	.. On anchored miss: accumulate the missed locator for post-loop refusal
39	.. {rbbc_fatal}
40	if any anchored miss accumulated, emit a remediation block pointing at {rbtgo_image_retrieve} for each missed locator and abort before invoking docker build
41	.. The credentialed fetch for anchored slots is deliberately out-of-band — kludge never authenticates against GAR. The inline origin pull reaches upstream without credentials and does not change this property.
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
45	.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	`«HALLMARK»`
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Build Image Locally*:
51	.. Validate the vessel Dockerfile against {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}: any column-0 `FROM` line must use `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`, or `scratch` as its image token. Abort before invoking `docker build` on violation.
52	.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
53	.. The no-network property comes from the presence check above (every slot verified in the local cache — origin auto-pulled if absent, anchored refused if absent) combined with buildx's default behavior of not re-pulling base images. {rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene} closes the residual hardcoded-`FROM` risk that the discontinued `--pull=never` flag used to backstop.
54	.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
55	.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format
56	
57	//axhos_step
58	. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
59	.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree (`«GAR_BASE»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`)
60	.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
61	.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)
62	
63	//axhos_step
64	. *Report Hallmark*:
65	.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
66	.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining
67	
68	== Limitations
69	
70	* Requires GCP depot configuration to construct GAR-formatted image tags (registry host, project, repository), but no credentials are consumed and no registry is contacted for hallmark publication — only origin-slot upstream pulls reach a registry, unauthenticated
71	* Anchored base images must be cached locally before kludge runs — fetched out-of-band via {rbtgo_image_retrieve}. Origin pass-through slots auto-pull inline if missing, so the cold-cache path for origin-only vessels is a single operator step; vessels with anchored slots remain two steps (wrest, then kludge).
72	* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
73	* No about artifact, no provenance chain -- the vouch alias carries no attestation content
74	* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks
75	
76	== Tabtarget
77	
78	`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`
79	
80	== See Also
81	
82	* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build; shares anchor-resolution semantics with kludge
83	* {rbtgo_image_retrieve} -- credentialed wrest of an enshrined base image into the local cache (remediation target when an anchored slot is missing)
84	* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
85	* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)
86

35	
36	  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'

53	# Image-Presence Predicate
54	#

56	# forensic temp file rather than the terminal. Returns docker's exit
57	# status; callers consume via `if zrbfk_image_present_predicate ...` or
58	# `... || ...`. The forensic files persist in BURD_TEMP_DIR for the
59	# command's lifetime, available for post-mortem inspection.
60	zrbfk_image_present_predicate() {
61	  zrbfk_sentinel
62	  local -r z_ref="${1:-}"
63	  test -n "${z_ref}" || buc_die "zrbfk_image_present_predicate: image ref required"

73	######################################################################
74	# Kludge Build - Local image build for development

77	# kludge hallmark (k-prefixed timestamp) in the same GAR-style

82	# Host platform only (no multi-arch).

84	rbfk_kludge() {

86

98	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)

103	  # Load vessel configuration

106	  # Validate conjure mode (bind and graft don't have local Dockerfiles)

112	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' has no RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT"
113	  test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
114	    || buc_die "Dockerfile not found: ${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"

120	  # Slot types diverge by credential need: anchored refs point into GAR and need
121	  # GCP auth, so misses refuse with a wrest remediation (credentialed-out-of-band).
122	  # Origin refs are public upstream and need no credentials, so misses auto-pull
123	  # inline. The presence guard's local-cache assertion is the tag-drift defense:
124	  # docker build (buildx default: no re-pull) runs against the cached image.
125	  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
126	  local z_build_args=()

128	  local z_origin_var=""
129	  local z_anchor_var=""

131	  local z_anchor=""
132	  local z_slot_ref=""
133	  local z_pkg_path=""

152	      esac
153	      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
154	      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
155	      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
156	      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
157	      z_slot_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_pkg_path}:${z_tag}"

159	      zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \

161	    else
162	      z_slot_ref="${z_origin}"
163	      buc_info "Image slot ${z_slot} (pass-through): ${z_slot_ref}"

167	          || buc_die "docker pull failed for origin slot ${z_slot}: ${z_slot_ref}"
168	        zrbfk_image_present_predicate "${z_slot_ref}" \
169	          || buc_die "Origin image still absent from local cache after pull: ${z_slot_ref}"

171	    fi

173	    z_build_args+=("--build-arg" "RBF_IMAGE_${z_slot}=${z_slot_ref}")
174	  done
175	  (( ${#z_build_args[@]} )) || buc_die "No RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN found in vessel config"
176	
177	  if (( ${#z_anchored_misses[@]} )); then
178	    buc_warn "Kludge cannot proceed — anchored base image(s) not cached locally"
179	    buc_bare "  Anchored slots point into GAR and require credentials. Kludge runs"
180	    buc_bare "  uncredentialed, so anchored images must be wrested into the local"
181	    buc_bare "  docker cache out-of-band before kludge runs."

195	  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree

198	  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).

202	  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
203	  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
204	  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"

206	  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds).
207	  # Buildx defaults to not re-pulling base images; the presence guard above has
208	  # already verified every slot is in the local cache, so the build never networks.
209	  buc_step "Building image locally"

211	    "${z_build_args[@]}" \

213	    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
214	    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \

217	  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
218	  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
219	  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
220	    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"
221	
222	  # Persist facts — mirror conjure/bind/graft so downstream consumers (theurge)
223	  # can build full refs uniformly regardless of mode.
224	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
225	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"
226	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"
227	
228	  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
229	  buc_bare ""
230	  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
231	  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
232	  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
233	  buc_bare ""
234	  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_about}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation assembles the
5	{rbtga_ark_about}
6	metadata artifact for an existing
7	{rbtga_ark_image}
8	in
9	{rbtgi_repository}.
10	The about scripts (`rbgja01`–`rbgja04`) are uniform across all three vessel modes and always execute as Cloud Build steps.
11	This procedure describes those steps — not a standalone Cloud Build job.
12	Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft combines these steps with vouch steps in a single about+vouch Cloud Build job.
13	All jobs run as the
14	{rbtr_mason}
15	service account on the private pool (`RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`).
16	
17	NOTE: The about steps receive their context via substitution variables (`_RBGA_*`) and workspace state, not via function parameters.
18	For conjure, the hallmark is read from `/workspace` (computed by the preceding build steps); for bind and graft, it comes from the `_RBGA_HALLMARK` substitution variable.
19	See <<rbtgo_about_substitutions>> below for the full variable reference.
20	
21	NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before about runs. The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.
22	
23	NOTE: The {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} is prepended at the entry-point level (`zrbfd_stitch_build_json`, `zrbfd_mirror_submit`, `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`); the about-step assembly itself does not inject it.
24	
25	//axhos_step
26	. *Cloud Build Step 1: Discover Platforms* (Python 3):
27	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
28	.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
29	.. {rbbc_call}
30	Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` with OCI media type accept headers
31	.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
32	... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
33	OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
34	Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
35	They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
36	Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
37	+
38	See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
39	... {rbbc_store}
40	`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
41	.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
42	... {rbbc_call}
43	GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
44	... {rbbc_store}
45	`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	platform count for subsequent step branching
48	.. After platform discovery, attempt diags artifact extraction (conjure-only):
49	... {rbbc_call}
50	Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
51	... If HTTP 200: fetch manifest, iterate layer blobs, extract diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`) to `/workspace`
52	... If HTTP 404 or error: skip gracefully (expected for bind/graft — no diags artifact exists)
53	.. NOTE: The diags artifact is pushed by conjure step 06 (`rbgjb06-push-diags.sh`) and contains build-time diagnostics that cannot be conveyed via Cloud Build substitution variables (Dockerfile content exceeding the ~4KB limit, BuildKit metadata, Docker daemon cache snapshots).
54	
55	//axhos_step
56	. *Cloud Build Step 2: Syft SBOM Generation*:
57	.. Image: docker (from reliquary; runs syft via `docker run` with reliquary syft image)
58	.. Fetch OAuth token via metadata server (syft authenticates as {rbtr_mason})
59	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
60	... Conjure with per-platform tags (multi-platform): {rbbc_call}
61	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»-«PLATFORM_SUFFIX»`
62	... Conjure single-platform (single manifest, not an index): {rbbc_call}
63	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»`
64	... Bind/graft (always via per-platform digest): {rbbc_call}
65	`syft registry:«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` using per-platform digest from step 1's manifest list
66	+
67	NOTE: Bind and graft images always use `@«PLATFORM_DIGEST»` pinning, even for single-platform images.
68	Grafted images may be stored as OCI image indexes containing attestation manifests (see step 1 filtering); scanning the main tag would cause syft to auto-select the Cloud Build worker's native platform (amd64) from the index, failing when the image contains only a different architecture.
69	Digest pinning bypasses index platform resolution entirely.
70	... {rbbc_store}
71	per-platform SBOM to `/workspace/sbom-«LABEL».json` (e.g., `sbom-amd64.json`; LABEL = architecture + variant without separators)
72	.. NOTE: Syft's `registry:` transport reads directly from the OCI registry — no docker CLI or local pull required.
73	
74	//axhos_step

76	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
77	.. For each platform in `«PLATFORMS»`:
78	... Construct `build_info.json` with mode-aware fields organized into nested JSON objects:
79	.... Root: `hallmark`, `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`

83	.... Bind `bind` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE`)
84	.... Graft `graft` object: `source` (from `_RBGA_GRAFT_SOURCE`)
85	... {rbbc_store}

87	.. Write `recipe.txt` using dual-source logic:
88	... If `recipe.txt` already exists in `/workspace` (written by diags extraction in step 1): skip substitution variable write, log that `recipe.txt` came from the diags artifact
89	... Else if `_RBGA_DOCKERFILE_CONTENT` is non-empty: {rbbc_store}
